steps.title: Identify the correction pattern
steps.content: Chaucer didn't write in Modern English, and the sentence shifts to what he actually did write (Middle English). The blank must bridge this negation to the actual fact—a direct correction.

steps.title: Match the corrective word
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast/correction: the sentence negates Modern English, then states the true alternative (Middle English). The blank needs a transition signaling "instead" or "on the contrary."
  • Choice A — "rather,": signals correction or substitution ("not X, rather Y"), so fits the shift from false to true. ✓
  • Choice B — "finally,": signals the last item in a sequence, so fails — no sequence is present. ✗
  • Choice C — "as a result,": signals cause-and-effect, so fails — not writing in Modern English did not cause him to write in Middle English. ✗
  • Choice D — "similarly,": signals agreement or likeness, so fails — the two clauses are contrasting, not alike. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
